---
title: "quartobot"
subtitle: "The manubot manuscript-as-software pattern, on Quarto."
toc: false
---

::: {.callout-note appearance="simple"}
**Status:** v0.1 scaffold in flight. The CLI installs today from
git (`uv tool install git+https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot`);
PyPI publication follows the v0.1 tag. Track
[v0.1 on the milestone board](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/milestone/1).
:::

Manubot has, for eight years, run scholarly manuscripts as a git
repository that builds itself on every commit, resolves citations from
DOIs and PubMed IDs automatically, hands out an immutable permalink per
commit, and collaborates through pull requests. The pattern is
[published](https://doi.org/10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128) and used in
hundreds of preprints.

Quarto now covers more of scholarly publishing than manubot ever did —
manuscripts, books, websites, slides, dashboards, courseware — but the
manubot pattern does not exist there natively. quartobot closes that
gap.

## What ships

Four artifacts, designed to work alone or together:

- **[A Python CLI](cli.qmd)** with `quartobot resolve` — invoked from
  Quarto's `project.pre-render:` hook — plus `scan`, `validate`, and
  `init`. Authors write `@doi:10.1371/journal.pcbi.1007128` in their
  prose; citations resolve before pandoc runs.
- **[A manuscript template](template.qmd)** combining Quarto, the
  pre-render hook wiring, and a CI workflow that gives every commit
  an immutable permalink at `/v/<sha>/`, embeds that permalink in
  rendered HTML, posts PR previews, and deploys HTML + PDF + DOCX
  to GitHub Pages.
- **[A book template](book.qmd)** for longer works (textbooks, reviews,
  edited volumes, theses). Same pattern as the manuscript template,
  but Quarto's book project type — sidebar navigation, full-text
  search, per-chapter HTML pages. The differentiator from manubot,
  which is manuscript-shaped by design.
- **Composite GitHub Actions + a reusable workflow** so consumer-side
  CI is ten lines, with upstream improvements flowing through version
  pins.

## Origin

[manubot/manubot#332](https://github.com/manubot/manubot/issues/332)
("Quarto integration") was opened by Anthony Gitter in April 2022 after
a conversation with Sean Davis. Four years later, no PR, no assignee —
but `pandoc-manubot-cite` shipped inside the `manubot` package and Quarto
Manuscripts shipped as a first-party project type. The integration is
small once you stop trying to rebuild the resolver. This site documents
the work to resolve that issue.

## Working example

[seandavi/2026-venice-spatial-hackathon-manuscript](https://github.com/seandavi/2026-venice-spatial-hackathon-manuscript)
runs the CI / permalink / banner half of the pattern on a live 25-author
preprint from the Bioconductor Spatial Hackathon. The template repo is
being lifted from it.

## Where to go next

- New to the project? Start with [Getting started](getting-started.qmd).
- Coming from a manubot manuscript?
  [Differences from manubot](differences-from-manubot.qmd) explains
  what carries over and what changes;
  [Migrating from manubot](migrating-from-manubot.qmd) is the
  step-by-step.
- Looking for design rationale? [Design](design.qmd) collects the
  decisions and the prior art.
- Want to contribute? See [`CONTRIBUTING.md`](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md)
  and the [open issues](https://github.com/seandavi/quartobot/issues).
